title: Synergistic Enhancement of AAV Gene Delivery in 2D Cells and 3D Organoids Using Polybrene and Hydroxychloroquine
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"acvjf","text":"Recent advances in three-dimensional (3D) culture platforms have enabled organoids to serve as physiologically relevant models for recapitulating human biology and assessing therapeutic efficacy and toxicity. Despite their promise, their complex architecture presents significant challenges for efficient gene delivery, thereby limiting their broader application in drug discovery and translational research. To overcome this challenge, we developed a sequential treatment strategy that combines polybrene (PB), which facilitates viral entry, and hydroxychloroquine (HCQ), which modulates endosomal processing. By applying PB as a pre-treatment and HCQ as a post-treatment, we achieved an approximate 1.3- to 2-fold increase in adeno-associated virus (AAV) transduction efficiency in both retinal and liver organoid models compared to single-agent treatments, and a 1.7- to 2.5-fold increases compared to treatment with virus alone. Importantly, this combinatorial treatment preserved cellular integrity, as confirmed by minimal TUNEL assay and high overall viability. Our findings demonstrate that sequential administration PB and HCQ significantly improves AAV transduction in 3D retinal and liver organoid systems, offering a robust method to improve gene delivery. This approach not only overcomes current limitations in organoid-based research but also supports the development of more predictive platforms for evaluating AAV vectors and advancing gene therapy application.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Polybrene
Sub-culture of normal cell line
Retinal organoids
Treatment of polybrene
Transduction of AAV to cell line and retinal organoids
Sample preparation for Flow cytometry
Sample preparation for immunofluorescence staining
Prepare autoclaved tertiary distilled water.
Dissolve the polybrene powder in tertiary distilled water to make a final concentration of 5 mg/ml ~ 10 mg/ml .
Hydroxychloroquine
Filter HCQ solution with 0.22 m filter.
Dissolve the HCQ tablet in tertiary distilled water to make a final concentration of 10 millimolar (mM) .
Aliquot HCQ stock into 1.5 mL sterile micro-centrifuge tubes.
Seeding of cell lines
Aspirate culture media and wash with 1X PBS.
Add 1 mL of 0.05% Trypsin-EDTA in the culture plate and incubate culture plate in 5% CO 2 , 37 °C culture incubator for approximately 00:02:00 .
Add 1 mL of 10DMEM medium and detach the cancer cell by tapping the plate with hands.
Transfer the detached cells to 15 mL conical tube and centrifuge it under 1000 rpm, °C, 3 minutes .
Aspirate supernatant and add 1 mL of 10DMEM medium to resuspend cells.
Seed a ratio of 1/5 of the cells into a new 100-mm cell culture dish with 10DMEM medium.
Repeat steps in Sub-culture of cell lines [ go to step #3.1 go to step #3.5 ].
Dilute 100 µL to 1/10 by adding 900 µL of fresh media, making a final volume of 1 mL .
Mix 20 µL of 1/10 diluted cell resuspension with 20 µL of Trypan Blue in a 1.5 mL sterile micro-centrifuge tube.
Clean a hematocytometer and load 10 µL of the sample in each side.
Count the cells and seed appropriate number of cells into a new 48-well plate.
Prepare retinal organoids and transfer retinal organoids to a new 96 well U-bottom or flat-bottom low attachment plate.
Change medium into fresh pre-warmed virus medium up to 200 µL .
Liver organoids
Prepare liver organoid differentiation medium (DM) and basal medium, and store them at 4 °C until use.
Dilute Matrigel in cold DPBS at a 1:100 ratio and coat a flat-bottom 24-well plate for 01:00:00 in a 37 °C incubator. (Keep the Matrigel On ice at 4 °C .)
Harvest the organoid embedded in Matrigel dome using a 1000 μL pipette tip, transfer it into microcentrifuge tubes, and briefly spin down using a mini centrifuge to pellet the organoids.
Remove the supernatant, resuspend the organoid pellet in basal medium, and briefly spin down again using a mini centrifuge.
After removing the supernatant, add cold Cell Recovery Solution (60 μL per dome) and incubate the cultures in Cell Recovery solution at 4 °C for approximately 00:10:00 .
Briefly centrifuge the cultures to separate the organoids from the Cell Recovery solution. Remove the supernatant and wash the cultures with cold DPBS twice.
Remove the supernatant and resuspend the organoids in DM (100 μL per well of a flat-bottom 24-well plate).

Add 400 µL of fresh DM to each well of the Matrigel-coated 24-well plate ( go to step #7.3 ), and seed the 12-15 organoids per well by adding 100 µL of organoid suspension (as prepared in go to step #7.8 ). (Additionally, prepare three wells of organoids for cell counting, to be used in go to step #11.1 below.)
To anchor the organoids (but not fully attached) to the bottom of the Matrigel-coated plate, incubate the organoids for 04:00:00 - 06:00:00 in a 37 °C , 5% CO 2 incubator.
16:00:00 to 18:00:00 after seeding an appropriate number of cells per well, treat with the polybrene at a concentration of 5 μg/ml ~ 10 μg/ml .
In the case of organoids, on the day organoids are transferred, polybrene can be treated at a concentration of of 5 μg/ml ~ 15 μg/ml .
About 01:00:00 to 04:00:00 after, AAV can be transduced in either cells or organoids.
Treatment of HCQ
16:00:00 to 18:00:00 after seeding an appropriate number of cells per well, treat with the HCQ at a concentration of 15 μM .
In the case of organoids, on the day organoids are transferred, HCQ at a concentration of 10 μM , 15 μM , 20 μM .
01:00:00 after, AAV can be transduced in either cells or organoids.
Transduction of AAV to liver organoids
Observe the morphology and condition of cells.

Dilute AAV in 1X PBS at the desired MOI. (Ex. 2×10 4 vg /cell or 1×10 10 vg /organoid) AAV stock needs to be diluted with the appropriate dilution factor. The Volume of transduced AAV should be over 1 µL to avoid pipetting errors.
Transduce the diluted AAV into the cells.
Incubate in a 37 °C , 5% CO 2 incubator.
To calculate the treatment concentration of AAVs, transfer organoids from three wells (n=3) as prepared in go to step #7.9 into microcentrifuge tubes, and briefly spin down using a mini centrifuge.
Remove the supernatant, resuspend the organoid pellet in basal medium, and spread the organoid suspension onto the cell culture dish.
To dissociate the organoids into single cells for counting, mechanically chop the organoids using a surgical blade.

Transfer the cells into microcentrifuge tube and incubate them with Trypsin-EDTA for 00:10:00 in a 37 °C incubator. Every 00:05:00 , pipette up and down using a 200 µL pipette tip to fully dissociate the cells into a single-cell suspension.
Count the number of cells using a standard hematocytometer or automated cell counter. (10–12 organoids per well is approximately equivalent to 1×10 5 cells per well.)

Change the medium to 250 µL of DM supplemented with 10 μM Y-27632 and the appropriate concentration of AAVs. (Ex, Use 1×10 1 to 1×10 3 vg /cell for evaluating AAV cell entry, and 1×10 6 vg /cell for transgene expression analysis.)
Incubate the organoids on a bi-directional rocker in a 37 °C , 5% CO 2 incubator.
Flow cytometry
Observe the morphology and condition of 2D cells.
Aspirate culture media and wash with 1X PBS.
Add 100 µL of 0.05% Trypsin-EDTA in the culture plate and incubate culture plate in 37 °C , 5% CO 2 incubator for approximately 00:02:00 .
Add 100 µL of 2% FBS in 1X PBS and detach the cells by tapping the plate with hands.
Pipette and transfer the detached cells to a 1.5 mL sterile micro-centrifuge tube.
Add 300 µL of 2% FBS in 1X PBS and strain using a 5 mL polystyrene round-bottom tube with a cell strainer cap.
Run the flow cytometry software.
Load the samples one by one.
Analyze the data.

Collect retinal organoid samples using a 2 mL pipette aid and transfer them to 1.5 mL sterile micro-centrifuge tube. In the case of liver organoids, follow the steps ( go to step #7.4 go to step #7.7 )
Section the samples at a thickness of 10 µm using a cryotome and mount three sections per silane-coated slide. The slides should be stored -20 °C unless staining is performed immediately.
Embed the samples in OCT compound and freeze them in a deep-freezer Overnight at -80 °C .
Remove the 15% sucrose and add 30% sucrose in 1X PBS, incubating Overnight at 4 °C .
Incubate the samples in 15% sucrose in 1X PBS for 01:00:00 at Room temperature .
Remove the 4% PFA and wash the samples 3 times with 1X PBS.
Remove the 1X PBS and add 1 mL of 4% paraformaldehyde (PFA), incubating for 01:00:00 at Room temperature .
Wash the samples twice with 1X PBS.
Immunofluorescence staining of slides
Wash the slide with tertiary distilled water to remove remaining OCT compound around the samples.
Let the slides dry in a dark place Overnight at Room temperature .
Apply 1~2 drops of mounting medium on each sample and cover with a cover glass.
Gently remove excess moisture around the samples.
Wash the slides in 0.1% PBST containing 1% FBS five times, for 00:10:00 each.
Gently remove excess moisture around samples and apply the diluted secondary antibody, incubating the slides in the humid chamber for 02:00:00 at Room temperature .
Dilute the secondary antibody and Hoechst with the appropriate dilution factor in the same solution used for the primary antibody.
Wash the slides in 0.1% PBST containing 1% FBS five times, for 00:10:00 each.
Aspirate the blocking and permeabilization buffer and apply the diluted primary antibody solution, incubating the slides in the humid chamber Overnight at 4 °C .
Dilute the primary antibody with the appropriate dilution factor in 1% FBS in 1X PBS.
Add blocking and permeabilization buffer consisting of 5% FBS with 0.1% Triton X-100 in 1X PBS and incubate for at least 00:30:00 at Room temperature in a humid chamber.
Create a barrier around the samples using a barrier pen.
Gently remove excess moisture around the samples using Kimtech wipes. The samples must not be dried.
TUNEL assay of slides
Imaging slides
Follow the manual of In Situ Cell Death Detection Kit, Fluorescein from Roche.
Run the imaging software.
Load the slides upside-down, ensuring cover glass faces microscope lenses.
Capture images at 10X to 40X magnification.
Aliquot polybrene stock into 1.5 mL sterile micro-centrifuge tubes.
Filter polybrene solution with 0.22 m filter.
Prepare autoclaved tertiary distilled water.
In the sequential treatment protocol for organoids, HCQ is applied for 36:00:00 to 48:00:00 post-viral transduction.
For AAV entry analysis, transfer the AAV-transduced organoids ( go to step #11.7 ) from the matrigel-coated plate into microcentrifuge tubes and wash organoids with DPBS three times.
Overlay the droplet with DM ( 500 µL per well for a 24-well plate) and incubate for 3–10 days in a 37 °C , 5% CO2 incubator. Change the medium every 2 days.
Incubate the cells for 5–10 minutes in a 37 °C incubator until the Matrigel is solidified.

For further incubation of the AAV-transduced organoids ( go to step #11.8 ), Resuspend organoids with Matrigel ( 30 µL per dome) and add the Matrigel droplet to the center of each well in a new flat-bottom 24-well plate.
The organoid pellet can be used for next generation sequencing (NGS) analysis or stored at -80 °C for later analysis.
In the case of retinal organoids, Incubate for 15 days in a 37 °C , 5% CO 2 incubator. Change the medium every 2 days.
Sub-culture of human Embryonic Stem Cells
Human embryonic stem cells (hESCs; H9) were maintained under feeder-free conditions using mTeSR1 medium on Matrigel-coated culture plates at 37 °C with 5% CO 2
The culture medium was refreshed on a daily basis.
For routine passaging, colonies were dissociated into small clumps with ReLeSR and reseeded at a split ratio of 1:10–1:20 approximately once per week.

Perform retinal organoids differentiation according to previously validated protocols. Human Pluripotent Stem Cell-Derived Retinal Organoids: A Viable Platform for Investigating the Efficacy of Adeno-Associated Virus Gene Therapy.. International journal of stem cells.

Perform liver organoids differentiation according to previously validated protocols. Efficient and reproducible generation of human induced pluripotent stem cell-derived expandable liver organoids for disease modeling.. Scientific reports. Generation of expandable human pluripotent stem cell-derived hepatocyte-like liver organoids.. Journal of hepatology.
Cell viability under these treatment conditions was assessed and found to remain above 80% (see Analysis for cell viability section).
Cell viability under these treatment conditions was assessed and found to remain above 80% (see Analysis for cell viability section).
Sample preparation for cell viability assay
Add the reagent at a final volume corresponding to 1/10 of the culture medium. (In our case, this was 20 µL per well in a 48-well plate containing 200 µL of medium.)
Incubate culture plate in 37 °C , 5% CO 2 incubator for approximately 01:00:00 .
Transfer 150 µL of medium containing the reagent into each well of a 96-well plate.
Measure the absorbance at 450 nm with a microplate reader.
Enter the absorbance reading from each well in the equation below to calculate the cell survival rate. Survival rate (%) = (A sample - A blank )/(A negative control - A blank )×100
Count and seed the cells to multi-well plates. Make sure to place a well of only media to measure the background.
